::: {.tikz-desc data-node="sdata"}
**Private data**: Sender's sensitive data, held privately and never shared.

$$x \in \mathsf{X}$$
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="mech"}
**Mechanism**: A (deterministic or randomised) mechanism applied by Sender to the data $x$ to produce the output.
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="sstat"}
**Statistic**: The released statistic produced by the mechanism and shared with the receiver.

$$T \sim M(x, \cdot)$$
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="strans"}
**Privacy guarantee**: We are trying to construct this. If it is independent of the data, it will not affect the data-posterior.
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="spriv"}
**Privacy function**: The realised privacy is

$$
\rho(d^{Q_T},x)
$$
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="adata"}
**Data-prior** $Q$ the receiver's assumed prior over the data.

$$Q \in \mathcal{P}(\mathsf{X})$$
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="apost"}
**Data-posterior.** Receiver's posterior given the release of $T$.

$$Q_T = Q(\,\cdot \mid T,M,\mathfrak{C}) = Q(\,\cdot \mid T,M)$$

Knowledge of $\mathfrak{C}$ does not affect the $Q_T$ by _Assumption 1_.
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="aact"}
**Receiver decision**: $d$, formed by posterior $Q_T$ and loss $\ell$.

$$
d^{Q_T} \in \arg\inf_{d \in \mathcal{D}} \mathbb{E}_{z \sim Q_T}[ \ell(d,z)]
$$

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="aloss"}
**Receiver loss**: Receiver's loss function. 

:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="statbox"}
**Sender**: Chooses $M$ to persuade the Receiver to make decisions that do not affect (Sender's) privacy.
:::

::: {.tikz-desc data-node="advbox"}
**Receiver**: Assumed to be a Bayesian agent (_Assumption 2_).
:::
